assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions TPU slices being terminated but doesn't specifically explain the atomic recreation process or contrast it with rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: While the response mentions all TPU slices will be affected, it doesn't clearly explain that maintenance on one slice triggers restart of all slices in the multislice setup

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that training job will be interrupted and TPU slices will be preempted/terminated

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response mentions TPU slice recreation will add downtime but doesn't explain the resource availability blocking issue

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends saving an extra checkpoint manually before the maintenance window in the pre-maintenance actions section

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes code examples for robust checkpointing and discusses restoration procedures, covering automatic resume capability

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions a maintenance window timeline, it doesn't discuss controlling when maintenance occurs or scheduling during planned breaks
